title: Introduction to TypeScript
content:
### What is TypeScript?

TypeScript is a strongly typed programming language that builds on JavaScript, giving you better tooling at any scale. It adds optional static typing, classes, interfaces, and other modern features to JavaScript.

### Why TypeScript?

| Feature | Benefit |
|---------|--------|
| Static Typing | Catch errors at compile time instead of runtime |
| Enhanced IDE Support | Better autocomplete, navigation, and refactoring |
| Improved Readability | Types serve as documentation |
| ECMAScript Features | Use latest JavaScript features compiled to older versions |
| Gradual Adoption | Can be adopted incrementally in existing JS projects |

### Who Uses TypeScript?

TypeScript is developed by Microsoft and used by millions of developers. Major companies like Google, Airbnb, and Slack use TypeScript in production.

> **Key Point:** TypeScript compiles to plain JavaScript, which means it runs anywhere JavaScript runs.

### TypeScript vs JavaScript

- TypeScript = JavaScript + a type system
- TypeScript needs to be compiled; JavaScript does not
- TypeScript catches type errors at compile time
- All JavaScript is valid TypeScript (but not the other way around)

quiz.question: What does TypeScript add on top of JavaScript?
